descriptions.value: First kingdom city established long ago, now overgrown and lost in the sands of time.
The center of power in the kingdom, few have ever seen its beauty.
Cities often send out scouts to patrol their borders, often in dangerous conditions.
More than one failed ruler has been banished to this cursed place.
Often thought to be a story for children, this scene depicts what is believed to be a utopia for Kings and Queens alike.

(from: 1G2C) Trapped in a Japanese burial ground surrounded by fierce locals and a wide array of weaponry, there's only one outcome Marcia can see that's valid - bloodbath!

(from: Dream Extension Finger) Clancy was gifted the most desirable superpower of all - the ability to extend the length of his finger beyond reasonable expectation - and got to work poking all of the critters he could set eyes on.

(from: Hi Me Ro Me) The Russian guy only known to the world as "Me Ro Me" is a thrillseeker in the near future, looking to conquer the tallest building in the world, a newly-erected, absolutely gigantic affair in the city of Hi, Japan, as its security sweeper attempts to eradicate him.

(from: Maddening Relapse 2) Sick of Mason Zane having all the fun going doolally in a hallucinated circular world, Daryl follows suit, running around in dizzying circles as all her life problems chase her.

(from: Japanese Infomercial) Shinzuke Gokowan, once an affluent television star, has been reduced to dressing up as a bottle of dish cleaner and appearing on low-budget infomercials trying to convince you that your dishes will never be the same again.

(from: Maddening series) Once a widely-regarded entrepreneur with no interests beyond the pursuit of wealth, an accidental stock market crash saw Mason Zane's reputation and his wealth destroyed. His response: to don the persona of Mason Zane, and see the world as a massively simplified metaphor of his life.

(from: Radslaughter) Tired of your hits being pulled off by boring, faceless people? Go to the Rad Model, knock thrice on door 024, and get them done by a dude that duel-wields rifles, using them not only to take out his targets but also to propel his skateboard.

(from: Lyssandra and the Amplegores) Professor Whiskery is the head cat of a feline laboratory that has accidentally created a continuous stream of synthetic beings called Amplegores. The lab creates a weaponised vehicle to aid in taking them out... then throws a dog into it, because they're cats.
(from: Frankenfirearm) Gorm are a species of blue horned monsters that secretly only really want a big hug. But their hugs are constrictive and deadly.
(from: Ne Touchez Pas series) Oiseau is a French bird that finds itself trapped in a series of claustrophobic corridors, and must fly through them with finesse without touching the walls.

(from: Titan Tighten) Titan is a gigantic machine built by the mastermind behind the Lab Wagon to level cities. Sadly, it's been built pretty shoddily, and it's up to the equally-goofy fixer droid Tighten to screw him back together from the inside.

Kimberley is a redheaded man in a dystopian future where hair colour of the sort is considered a religion, and infidels attempt to take you down for your genetically-forced alliance. Kimberley has a pair of lethal tazers, though, and he's more than happy to cook up a batch of his famous infidel toasties.
